TextENGNew: I already knew there would be no club activities after school today — Mion hadn't even needed to say so.

Shion had invited me today.
...All-you-can-eat desserts!!
I'd deliberately left half my lunch untouched, so between the anticipation and my empty stomach, I was at peak readiness!
"Keiichi-kun, you look happy today! Did something good happen? Did it?!"

Of course she noticed.
Rena asked with a happy tilt of her head.
I could invite Rena to the dessert buffet too, couldn't I?
...Hmm, but I was pretty sure Shion had said something about having a ticket...
For all I knew, today's event might be ticketed entry only, with no way to get in without one...
If that were the case, inviting Rena only to have her turned away at the door would be too cruel...
"Yeah. Just a little stroke of luck. I'll fill you in tomorrow!! You're going to be so jealous, Rena~!!!"
"Wha, wha, wha! What is it, what is it! I'm so jealous~! Is this lucky thing... adorable? Is it?"
...Adorable?
...Hmm, I wonder — what would Rena make of the Angel Mort uniforms?
...She'd be completely annihilated. Without question.
"...Probably. It might be very adorable, actually..."
"Ehhhh!!! I'm so jealous~!! What is it, what is it?!"

"Oh? What kind of luck did you stumble into?
...Hm? Wait — did you pull a lucky ticket at Angel Mort about two months ago? And you won?"
"Hey, I hadn't even transferred here two months ago."
"Oh, right. ...Then it's probably not the Angel's Dessert Festa either..."
"Mii-chan, what's the Angel's Dessert Festa?"
changeReason: Old says 'what Shion explained' treating them as separate people; new says 'she had given me last night as Shion' reflecting the JPN's framing that Mion and Shion are the same person (詩音として).

TextENGNew: "You know that family restaurant right in front of Okinomiya Station — Angel Mort?"
"Every season they hold a special event to unveil new desserts. That's today. ...Oh, but don't bother going. The whole restaurant is reserved — only ticket winners can get in."
changeReason: Old omits the description of tickets as マニア垂涎のプラチナチケット (platinum tickets coveted by enthusiasts); new includes this vivid detail from the Japanese.

TextENGNew: Mion gave me the same explanation she had given me last night as Shion.
"Rena should start going to the restaurant more! I'll work hard to win a ticket and aim for the next all-you-can-eat event!"
"Ahahaha, good luck! Oh, and word has it the winning tickets are platinum-grade collectibles that true aficionados drool over — they're supposedly going for crazy prices on the black market...!!"
...Hearing that made me appreciate the ticket Shion had arranged for me even more.
I'd thought of it as nothing more than a free dessert... but it turned out to be a remarkably precious ticket.
...Yet another thing I owed Shion a thank-you for...
"Ah... thank you. I had no idea it was worth that much..."

In a voice barely above a breath — just enough for Mion to hear.
...That was how I let it out.
Mion peered into my eyes for just a moment... then tilted her head slightly and smiled.
"...Hm? What? Did you say something?"
changeReason: Old's 'it would be a problem if you said something right now' misses the identity nuance; new captures 今の私に (the current me/this version of me), reflecting that gratitude should go to her Shion persona, not her Mion persona.

TextENGNew:
That smile seemed to be saying: don't tell me things like that while I'm being me.
...That was what I felt from it.
Of course. Thanking Mion while she was being Mion was never going to work.
I'd go to the restaurant.
And I would say a proper thank-you to the Mion who was Shion.
"Well then, this old man will take his leave. Today's shift is going to be pretty brutal~! See ya~!!"
Mion waved energetically as she headed off.
"Hey, Keiichi-kun. ...Mii-chan has been acting strange lately. Like she's floating or something, happy about something."
"...Hm... i-is that so?"
Rena had a surprisingly sharp eye for these things.
To me, Mion had seemed no different from usual... but Rena had apparently sensed something.
"Keiichi-kun doesn't know? I was sure you'd know..."
Rena smiled that teasing smile of hers, gave a little spin, and let her skirt billow.
"Know what... exactly..."
...It came out sounding evasive. But it wasn't a lie.
Honestly, I didn't know anything.
If anything, I was the one who wanted to know why Mion split herself into Shion and showed up in front of me.
It was a transparent pause — obviously guilty — but Rena didn't seem bothered.
"Oh, I see. I wonder what it is, then. But when Mii-chan is in a good mood, Rena is happy too."
She spread her arms wide and spun in place with a look of pure delight.

For some reason, Rena was in exceptionally high spirits.
...High enough that I couldn't help smiling along.
Even the cicadas sounded happy.
"But, you see..."
Rena's dancing spin stopped abruptly, and her eyes settled into something quieter.

"...You know, Mii-chan.
Just recently.
...She was really badly hurt."
"Hurt... ...huh?"
The hurt Rena meant wasn't physical — that much was clear.

"...At first she was pretending not to care.
But it was like an injury.
It swelled.
It throbbed and swelled."
changeReason: Old uses 'fester' (implying infection); new correctly uses 'swelled' and 'throbbed and swelled' matching 腫れてきた / ジンジン腫れてきた, which describes swelling, not festering.

TextENGNew:
"...Eventually she couldn't hold it in anymore, and she woke up in the middle of the night.
And then she called Rena.
...Mii-chan was crying."
"S-Sorry... what are you even talking about?"
"What am I talking about? Hm? ...Was Rena saying something just now? Was she?"
"H-Hey, don't play dumb like that! You just said...!"
"Don't know, don't know, don't knooow. Rena doesn't know anything. Hau~~~~..."
She laughed innocently and spun and spun until the dizziness finally caught up with her, and she staggered into the wall to catch herself.

"Hau~~~...
Everything's... spinning~~~..."
"What an idiot! That's what you get for spinning yourself silly!"
"S-Sorry... Hau... I might be a little sick..."
I lent my shoulder to the fool.
Staggering around like this at her age — truly impressive, in its own way.
After that, Rena was all bright spirits and incoherent babbling.
I laughed along, teasing her and playing straight man by turns.
But the chance to circle back to what Rena had said before never came.
"...So, Keiichi-kun."
Rena's dancing spin halted abruptly, and her eyes grew still.
"...Do you really not know why Mii-chan has been the way she has?"
Rena said nothing and looked straight into my eyes.
...You do know the reason Mii-chan has been so upbeat lately, don't you...?
Rena's eyes bore into me as if she could see right through everything.
But the truth was I really didn't understand the relationship between Mion and Shion. What could I say when I genuinely didn't know? If anything, I wanted Rena to enlighten me.
"...I-I really don't know."
What was that sigh supposed to mean...?! I genuinely don't know, so there's nothing I can do about it!
"Mii-chan really does have it tough ahead of her~~. Rena feels just a tiny bit sorry for her. ...A tiny bit."
"...What is that supposed to mean?!"
"Don't know, don't know, don't knooow. Rena doesn't know anything~~~~..."
As if to dodge my question, Rena started spinning again.
Rena's skirt fluttered and danced before my eyes.
Watching Rena spin without really watching, I let my thoughts drift to Mion.
...When Shion and I got along well, Mion's mood improved. ...But if I brought up what happened with Shion when Mion was being Mion, she barely reacted.
Why did Mion switch between two personas like this...? Wasn't it just a bother...? Or was there some deeper reason behind it?
...Just what on earth was going on?
"Rena... if you know something, tell me. ...Rena... hey...?"
"Don't knooow. Rena doesn't know a single thing~~."
...I'm the one who wants answers here!
In the end, what Rena had really been trying to tell me — as she kept on spinning — remained a complete mystery.
